subject: Re: From Joy Sohn Assistant to John Sykes
body:
it's ok

--- Rahna Jalashgar <rjalashgar@nysgmail.com> wrote:

> They were just calling to verify that she worked for
> you and that you
> would like Mr. Sykes to set up a meeting for her at
> MTV.  Please advise.
> Rahna
> 
> 
> ________________________________
> 
> From: Sohn, Joy - MTVN [mailto:Joy.Sohn@mtvn.com] 
> Sent: Thursday, June 01, 2006 2:09 PM
> To: Rahna Jalashgar
> Subject: FW: from Jeffrey Epstein's office
> 
> 
> Hi Rhana, 
>  
> Thank you for your help.  Whenever we get requests
> like this, we make a
> habit of verifying the information, especially if we
> haven't been
> notified to expect this person to contact us...just
> in case.  John will
> be more than happy to set her up with a meeting if
> in fact Mr. Epstein
> would like for us to do so.  Please let me know.  
>  
> Best regards,
> Joy
>  
> ________________________________________________ 
> 
> joy sohn 
> assistant to john sykes 
> 
> mtv networks 
> 1515 broadway, 47th floor, new york, ny  10036 
> t: 212.846.7880 | f: 212.846-6721 | e:
> joy.sohn@mtvn.com 
> ________________________________________________ 
> 
> 
> ________________________________
> 
> From: Jen Doyle [mailto:jdoyle@nysgmail.com] 
> Sent: Monday, May 22, 2006 5:46 PM
> To: Sohn, Joy - MTVN
> Subject: from Jeffrey Epstein's office
> 
> 
> 
> Dear Ms. Sohn,
> 
>  
> 
> My name is Jen Doyle and I am one of Jeffrey
> Epstein's assistants.  I
> have been with Jeffrey for nearly two years but have
> decided recently
> that I would like to leave his employment and pursue
> my passion: music.
> Jeffrey has been incredibly supportive of my
> decision, and as such he
> has graciously offered to help me look into a career
> in the music
> industry.  In particular, I expressed to him, that
> my greatest dream is
> to become an MTV VJ...and while Jeffrey and I both
> realize that this is
> an incredibly coveted and difficult position to come
> by, he offered to
> let me contact Mr. Sykes's office, on his behalf. 
> ...so, with his
> blessing, I contacted you on Friday evening and per
> your request, here
> is an email describing myself/interests, in greater
> detail.  
> 
>  
> 
> I realize that Mr. Sykes has an incredibly busy
> schedule, but first and
> foremost,  I would be ecstatic if he and/or anyone
> else at MTV, would be
> willing to sit down with me and discuss my career
> aspirations in music,
> and in particular, in being a part of  music
> television.   As noted, I
> am most interested in becoming a VJ and while I
> realize that only about
> 4-6 people in the entire nation, get to take on this
> role, at any given
> time - if there would be anyone willing to meet with
> me (such as a
> casting director, etc.), with respect to this
> interest, I would be
> unbelievably grateful.  I'd be very interested to
> have someone take a
> look at me, feel out my personality and furthermore,
> hear how passionate
> and knowledgeable I am about music.  If I am not
> right for the role
> and/or there are no position openings, I completely
> understand but I
> promised myself that I would give this dream, my
> best shot, and so... I
> am asking!  
> 
>  
> 
> If that doesn't work out, I am still extremely
> interested in working for
> MTV...I have attached my resume and as you will see,
> one of my majors in
> college was Political Science.  I love studying
> politics and current
> events, so maybe there would be something for me, in
> MTV News...?   or,
> I am currently an Executive Personal Assistant and
> enjoy my
> responsibilities, tremendously...perhaps there is an
> assistant role at
> MTV, which would suit me.  I am just throwing ideas
> out, but would be
> thrilled to discuss any/all of these possibilities
> with someone from
> your office.  
> 
>  
> 
> Thank you for your consideration and I appreciate
> your response.
> 
>  
> 
> Jen Doyle
> 
>  
> 
>  
> 
>

body_html:
it's ok

--- Rahna Jalashgar rjalashgar@nysgmail.com wrote:

They were just calling to verify that she worked for
you and that you
would like Mr. Sykes to set up a meeting for her at
MTV. Please advise.
Rahna

From: Sohn, Joy - MTVN [mailto:Joy.Sohn@mtvn.com]
Sent: Thursday, June 01, 2006 2:09 PM
To: Rahna Jalashgar
Subject: FW: from Jeffrey Epstein's office

Hi Rhana,

Thank you for your help. Whenever we get requests
like this, we make a
habit of verifying the information, especially if we
haven't been
notified to expect this person to contact us...just
in case. John will
be more than happy to set her up with a meeting if
in fact Mr. Epstein
would like for us to do so. Please let me know.

Best regards,
Joy

joy sohn
assistant to john sykes

mtv networks
1515 broadway, 47th floor, new york, ny 10036
t: 212.846.7880 | f: 212.846-6721 | e:
joy.sohn@mtvn.com

From: Jen Doyle [mailto:jdoyle@nysgmail.com]
Sent: Monday, May 22, 2006 5:46 PM
To: Sohn, Joy - MTVN
Subject: from Jeffrey Epstein's office

Dear Ms. Sohn,

My name is Jen Doyle and I am one of Jeffrey
Epstein's assistants. I
have been with Jeffrey for nearly two years but have
decided recently
that I would like to leave his employment and pursue
my passion: music.
Jeffrey has been incredibly supportive of my
decision, and as such he
has graciously offered to help me look into a career
in the music
industry. In particular, I expressed to him, that
my greatest dream is
to become an MTV VJ...and while Jeffrey and I both
realize that this is
an incredibly coveted and difficult position to come
by, he offered to
let me contact Mr. Sykes's office, on his behalf.
...so, with his
blessing, I contacted you on Friday evening and per
your request, here
is an email describing myself/interests, in greater
detail.

I realize that Mr. Sykes has an incredibly busy
schedule, but first and
foremost, I would be ecstatic if he and/or anyone
else at MTV, would be
willing to sit down with me and discuss my career
aspirations in music,
and in particular, in being a part of music
television. As noted, I
am most interested in becoming a VJ and while I
realize that only about
4-6 people in the entire nation, get to take on this
role, at any given
time - if there would be anyone willing to meet with
me (such as a
casting director, etc.), with respect to this
interest, I would be
unbelievably grateful. I'd be very interested to
have someone take a
look at me, feel out my personality and furthermore,
hear how passionate
and knowledgeable I am about music. If I am not
right for the role
and/or there are no position openings, I completely
understand but I
promised myself that I would give this dream, my
best shot, and so... I
am asking!

If that doesn't work out, I am still extremely
interested in working for
MTV...I have attached my resume and as you will see,
one of my majors in
college was Political Science. I love studying
politics and current
events, so maybe there would be something for me, in
MTV News...? or,
I am currently an Executive Personal Assistant and
enjoy my
responsibilities, tremendously...perhaps there is an
assistant role at
MTV, which would suit me. I am just throwing ideas
out, but would be
thrilled to discuss any/all of these possibilities
with someone from
your office.

Thank you for your consideration and I appreciate
your response.

Jen Doyle
